description: This figure is a time-temperature transformation diagram illustrating γ (austenite) phase decomposition behavior of Fe–28Mn–8.5Al–1C–1.2Si low-density steel during cooling/aging treatment. The x-axis represents time (range: 0.01–1000 h), and the y-axis represents temperature (range: 300–1000 °C). Two cooling regimes are marked: fast cooling (retains single γ phase at low temperature, with phase region γ+κ' at intermediate temperature) and slow cooling (triggers sequential γ decomposition). A threshold cooling rate of -4.4 °C/h is marked as the boundary for notable γ decomposition. Identified phase regions include single γ phase, γ+κ', γ+κ*, γ+κ'+κ*, γ+κ*+α(B2/DO₃), and γ+κ+B2/DO₃, with ordered phase intervals (high-temperature B2, intermediate B2+DO₃, low-temperature DO₃) labelled on the right. The core conclusion is that cooling rates slower than 4.4 °C/h induce precipitation of κ carbides and other secondary phases from the γ matrix, so adjusting cooling rate can control phase evolution, microstructure and final mechanical properties of the low-density steel.
